text:
There once was a little old lady named Matilda who lived in a cozy cottage on the edge of a moonlit forest.

Every evening after dinner, Matilda would wander through the woods, listening to the birds, feeling the gentle breeze, and smelling the blooming flowers.

One night, as she was walking, a soft, humming sound caught her attention. She followed it and found herself in a clearing. In the center stood an old oak tree.

The tree began to hum again, slowly growing bigger until it opened wide, revealing a beautiful singing bird. It hummed a melody so sweet that Matilda couldn't stop listening.

The bird sang:

"I have three cherry pits.

You can't swallow them.

They'll make you into a rainbow.

Then you can fly."

Matilda felt a warmth spreading through her.

After hearing the whole wishful song, she hummed back. A few moments later, she felt a fluttering sensation.

She had butterflies!

They danced around her, leaving
